name: The Transformation
description: A character undergoes profound internal or external change that redefines identity or worldview.

nodes.label: Fixed Identity
nodes.definition: The protagonist has a settled sense of who they are — their role, their capabilities, their place in the world. This identity may be comfortable or constraining, but it is stable.
entry_conditions: Audience sees the character's established identity and its limits
exit_conditions: A force or event begins to destabilize the fixed identity
typical_variants: Professional identity (I am a scientist, a soldier, a mother)
Social identity (I am normal, I am powerful, I am invisible)
Self-concept (I am capable, I am broken, I am in control)
failure_modes: Identity is so vaguely drawn that its transformation won't be visible
Identity is presented as already unstable — no baseline for change
signals_in_text: Others treating the character according to the identity
The character asserting who they are

nodes.label: Catalyst of Change
nodes.definition: An event, encounter, or internal shift begins the process of transformation. Something happens that the fixed identity cannot accommodate — a new power, a loss, a revelation, or a physical change.
entry_conditions: An external or internal force disrupts it
exit_conditions: The character's identity is destabilized; the old self is no longer tenable
typical_variants: Gaining a supernatural ability
Suffering a physical or psychological trauma
Encountering a radically different worldview
Being placed in a context that makes the old identity impossible
failure_modes: Catalyst is too minor to justify transformation
Catalyst is so overwhelming the character is passive rather than struggling

nodes.label: Denial and Struggle
nodes.definition: The protagonist resists the transformation, trying to maintain the old identity. They may hide the change, fight it, or pretend it isn't happening.
entry_conditions: The character's identity is under threat
exit_conditions: Denial fails — the transformation cannot be reversed or hidden
typical_variants: Hiding new abilities or changes from others
Seeking a cure or reversal
Attempting to force the old identity onto new circumstances
Lashing out at those who acknowledge the change
failure_modes: Denial is perfunctory — the character adapts too quickly
Denial is so extended the story stalls

nodes.label: Liminal State
nodes.definition: The protagonist exists between identities — no longer who they were, not yet who they will become. This is a state of maximum vulnerability and disorientation.
entry_conditions: The old identity is gone but no new identity has formed
exit_conditions: A crisis demands that the character act from their new self
typical_variants: Social isolation as neither world accepts them
Physical in-between state (half-transformed)
Psychological dissolution — not knowing who they are
Exile from former life without a new belonging
failure_modes: Liminal state is too comfortable — no urgency to resolve it
Character skips the in-between and is immediately settled in the new identity

nodes.label: Crucible
nodes.definition: A crisis forces the protagonist to act decisively from whatever they are becoming. The old identity cannot solve this problem — only the emerging self can. This is the moment that forges the new identity.
entry_conditions: The liminal state has left the character vulnerable
A threat or challenge demands action
exit_conditions: The protagonist acts from the new identity — successfully or catastrophically
typical_variants: Using new abilities to save others despite fear
Making a choice that only the new self would make
Confronting someone who embodies the old identity
Public display of the transformed self
failure_modes: Crucible can be solved by the old identity — no transformation was needed
The new identity succeeds effortlessly — no struggle in the moment
signals_in_text: Others seeing the character for who they now are

nodes.label: Integration
nodes.definition: The protagonist integrates the old and new selves into a coherent identity. They are not simply different — they are more complex, carrying the past into a new way of being.
entry_conditions: The character has proven the new identity under pressure
exit_conditions: The character has a stable, integrated sense of self
typical_variants: Accepting the transformation as permanent and meaningful
Finding a way to honor the old self within the new identity
Others accepting the transformed character
Teaching or helping others through similar transitions
failure_modes: Integration erases the old self entirely — no continuity
Integration is purely triumphant with no sense of loss
The character is unchanged despite everything — the transformation was cosmetic
signals_in_text: New relationships formed on honest terms
The character expressing who they are now

nodes.label: New Identity
nodes.definition: The protagonist inhabits their transformed self with stability and purpose. The world around them reflects the change — new roles, new relationships, new understanding.
typical_variants: New role that fits the transformed character
Transformed relationship to the community
Quiet self-knowledge and acceptance
Using the transformation to serve others
failure_modes: New identity is identical to the old one — nothing really changed
New identity is so foreign the audience doesn't recognize the character
signals_in_text: The character in their new role
Others responding to the transformed person

edges.label: Change Begins
edges.preconditions: Fixed identity is established; a destabilizing force arrives
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character's sense of self is threatened
edges.effects_on_character: Confusion, fear, or excitement as the old self starts to crack
edges.common_alternatives: Catalyst is sudden (accident, power); catalyst is gradual (environment, relationship)
edges.anti_patterns: Catalyst is too minor to shake a settled identity

edges.label: Resists the Change
edges.preconditions: The catalyst has occurred; the character's instinct is to preserve the old self
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each failed attempt to maintain the old identity deepens the crisis
edges.effects_on_character: Frustration and fear as control slips away
edges.common_alternatives: Resistance is active (seeking a cure); resistance is passive (pretending nothing happened)
edges.anti_patterns: Character never resists — accepts transformation immediately

edges.label: Falls Between Identities
edges.preconditions: Denial has failed; the old identity is no longer functional
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character is maximally vulnerable — no identity to fall back on
edges.common_alternatives: Liminal state is entered suddenly (identity collapses); entered gradually (erosion)
edges.anti_patterns: Character goes directly from denial to a settled new identity with no in-between

edges.label: Forged by Crisis
edges.preconditions: The character is in the liminal state; a threat demands action
edges.effects_on_stakes: The emerging identity is tested under maximum pressure
edges.effects_on_character: The new self is born through action rather than contemplation
edges.common_alternatives: Crisis is external (danger to others); crisis is internal (final psychological break)
edges.anti_patterns: Crisis can be solved without engaging the transformation at all

edges.label: Integrates Old and New
edges.preconditions: The crucible is survived; the character has acted as their new self
edges.effects_on_stakes: The existential crisis resolves — the character knows who they are
edges.effects_on_character: Continuity is established between past and present self
edges.common_alternatives: Integration is joyful; integration is bittersweet; integration requires mourning the old self
edges.anti_patterns: Old self is completely erased rather than integrated

edges.label: Settles Into New Self
edges.preconditions: Integration is complete; the character has a stable sense of self
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes dissolve into a new normal
edges.effects_on_character: The character is more complex than they were — carrying both selves
edges.common_alternatives: New identity is celebrated; new identity is quietly inhabited
edges.anti_patterns: New identity is indistinguishable from the old one
